tasks.instruction: Przeczytaj ponizszy tekst. Zaznacz, ktore zdania (1.1-1.4) sa zgodne z trescia tekstu (T – True), ktore sa niezgodne (F – False), a ktore zawieraja informacje niepodane w tekscie (NI – No Information). Nastepnie odpowiedz pelnym zdaniem na pytanie 1.5.
tasks.text:
POLAR BEARS

Polar bears are the largest land predators on Earth. Adult males can weigh up to 700 kilograms and stand nearly three metres tall on their hind legs. They live in the Arctic region, where temperatures can drop below minus 40 degrees Celsius, and have evolved remarkable adaptations to survive in this extreme environment.

Contrary to what many people think, polar bear fur is not actually white. Each hair is a hollow, transparent tube that appears white because it reflects visible light. Underneath their fur, polar bears have black skin, which helps them absorb heat from the sun. They also have a thick layer of fat, up to 11 centimetres deep, that insulates their bodies against the cold and provides energy when food is scarce.

Polar bears are excellent swimmers and can cover distances of over 100 kilometres without rest, using their large front paws as paddles. They rely on sea ice as a platform for hunting seals, which are their primary source of food. A polar bear will wait patiently beside a breathing hole in the ice for hours, sometimes even days, until a seal surfaces for air.

Climate change poses the greatest threat to polar bear survival. As global temperatures rise, Arctic sea ice is melting earlier in the spring and forming later in the autumn, giving polar bears less time to hunt. Some bears have been observed swimming increasingly long distances to find ice, which uses up valuable energy reserves. Scientists estimate that the global polar bear population could decline by more than 30 percent within the next three decades if current warming trends continue.

Several countries, including Canada, Norway, and Russia, have introduced measures to protect polar bears. International hunting quotas have been established, and some areas of the Arctic have been designated as protected habitats.

items.statement: Polar bears' fur keeps them warm partly because of its colour.
items.explanation: FALSE — The fur is not actually white; each hair is 'a hollow, transparent tube that appears white because it reflects visible light'. It is their black skin underneath and thick layer of fat that help with warmth, not the fur's colour.

items.statement: Polar bears have been known to wait for several days for a single hunting opportunity.
items.explanation: TRUE — The text states a polar bear 'will wait patiently beside a breathing hole in the ice for hours, sometimes even days, until a seal surfaces for air'.

items.statement: The number of polar bears has already fallen significantly compared to fifty years ago.
items.explanation: NO INFORMATION — The text gives a future prediction ('could decline by more than 30 percent within the next three decades') but does not provide data about past population changes.

items.statement: Climate change forces polar bears to use more energy than they would normally need for finding food.
items.explanation: TRUE — The text says bears 'have been observed swimming increasingly long distances to find ice, which uses up valuable energy reserves'.

openQuestion.question: Explain how the melting of sea ice affects polar bears' ability to find food.
openQuestion.modelAnswer: Polar bears use sea ice as a platform for hunting seals. When the ice melts earlier and forms later, they have less time to hunt. They also have to swim longer distances to find ice, which uses up their energy reserves.

vocabItems.definition: an animal that naturally hunts and kills other animals for food
physical or behavioural changes that help a living thing survive in its environment
allowing light to pass through so that objects on the other side can be clearly seen
to protect something from heat or cold by surrounding it with a material that prevents temperature loss
officially set aside for a particular purpose
